observation.summary: Harness and QA evidence could surface gaps, but the relationship between loops, business claims, code files, docs, verifier tokens, and evidence artifacts was still inferred.

hypothesis.summary: A Loop Registry SSOT can force business claims and semantically bound files to move with executable evidence instead of relying on agents to reread prose.

execution.summary: Validate registry loops, evidence graph closure, business claim file bindings, verifier source tokens, CI wiring, pre-commit wiring, generated docs, and optional changed-file co-change input.

evaluation.summary: The verifier fails on missing loop fields, missing code/doc/test/evidence anchors, workflow/pre-commit drift, generated doc drift, or runtime-only changes to claim-bound files.

retrospective.summary: The registry does not replace harnesses; it decides which harness discoveries are closed loops and which semantic files must co-change.

evidence.proves: loop registry claims are bound to files, docs, tests, CI, and pre-commit
public CI uploads loop registry evidence artifacts
